title: A problematic image on docker.io (python:2.7.9)
body:
### Issue Description

When building a Containerfile/Dockerfile based on `docker.io/library/python:2.7.9` (yes it's old!) podman (or buildah) is not able to cache (or reuse the cache?) of the image's layers. This problem does not occur on recent images.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Containerfile reproduce the issue:

```
FROM python:2.7.9
RUN echo "Hello world"
RUN echo "Goodbye"
```

First build:
```
STEP 1/3: FROM python:2.7.9
STEP 2/3: RUN echo "Hello world"
Hello world
--> af73a0e858db
STEP 3/3: RUN echo "Goodbye"
Goodbye
COMMIT test
--> 6d051101f972
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
6d051101f972401ae98cd1fb02c4c96c56284c23b75039aca099d9a71ae3e4fd
```

Second build:
```
STEP 1/3: FROM python:2.7.9
STEP 2/3: RUN echo "Hello world"
Hello world
--> d29db6160a13
STEP 3/3: RUN echo "Goodbye"
Goodbye
COMMIT test
--> 51f3d4ce8506
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
51f3d4ce85064aa3d9b18e1d70cba5a4522b0cd2b0c72412616e78e5c7807fe1
```

If another image is used, the second build looks like this:
```
STEP 1/3: FROM python:latest
STEP 2/3: RUN echo "Hello world"
--> Using cache 5bce20776ed03f0e0817641c071b847b38499ef9ce58b0e76bf6d1bff7854ab8
--> 5bce20776ed0
STEP 3/3: RUN echo "Goodbye"
--> Using cache 6b3f4695ba37c42d11ee50145eb45e566ead8f8aa38bf115010d3c442e28ef8b
COMMIT test
--> 6b3f4695ba37
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
6b3f4695ba37c42d11ee50145eb45e566ead8f8aa38bf115010d3c442e28ef8b
```

As expected the layers cache is working in this case:
```
--> Using cache 5bce20776ed03f0e0817641c071b847b38499ef9ce58b0e76bf6d1bff7854ab8
--> Using cache 6b3f4695ba37c42d11ee50145eb45e566ead8f8aa38bf115010d3c442e28ef8b
```

### Describe the results you received

Intermediate layers are not cached

### Describe the results you expected

Caching of the intermediate layers

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

If the image (python:2.7.9) is saved and loaded using `podman save` and `podman load` the problem does not occurs.

But still `podman manifest inspect` does not like this image:

```
$ podman manifest inspect python:2.7.9
Error: parsing manifest blob "{
...
}" as a "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+prettyjws": Treating single images as manifest lists is not implemented
```

Nor `podman image inspect python:2.7.9`:
```
...
          "RootFS": {
               "Type": "layers",
               "Layers": [
                    "",
                    "",
                    "",
                    "",
                    "",
                    "",
                    "",
                    "",
                    "",
                    "",
                    "",
                    "",
                    ""
               ]
          },
...
```

Nor the dockerhub since it does not display the image layers: https://hub.docker.com/layers/library/python/2.7.9/images/sha256-003095c76fac4d0d7d9437e2ba859bd2b5e1fb3459dc2b4f8c74bea0aa7bbc18?context=explore

comments.body: this has to be a result of how the image was built (circa 2015) and the standards used there? @mtrmac agree?

That image is schema1, rare nowadays, and with limited capabilities, supported in principle.

`podman manifest inspect` applies to multi-arch images, this isn’t one, so that’s not expected to work; the error message could be prettier.

If `podman image inspect` succeeds overall, the `RootFS` data is really not present, and not necessarily a problem.

As for whether that should preclude build caching, that’s a Buildah question and not something I immediately know.
Given I have not seen reports about other images I am going to close this as wontfix
